name: Chicken and Rice in a Sandy Clay Plot
recipeIngredient: 6 Chicken thighs, preferably with the skin
1 tbsp Fermented bean curd (fu yu), mashed (optional)
4 Green onions, including the green tops, cut into 2-inch lengths
2 tbsp Rice wine or dry sherry
recipeInstructions:
1. For entertaining, this recipe makes a fun presentation when prepared in four individual two-cup clay pots. To season a new sandy clay pot and lid, submerge both in cold water overnight.
2. Rinse with cold water until water runs clear. Drain thoroughly.
3. Put rice and water into a 2 1/2 to 3-quart sandy clay pot; set aside.
4. In 2 separate bowls, cover the mushrooms and lily buds with water until soft and pliable, about 30 minutes. Drain. Cut off and discard mushroom stems; cut caps in half. Cut off and discard hard ends of each lily bud; tie each bud into a knot. Set aside.
5. Using a heavy cleaver, chop the chicken crosswise into 3 pieces cleanly through the skin, meat, and bone, leaving no jagged chopped bones.
6. Toss chicken with marinade; set aside for 20 minutes.
7. Cut sausages into thin diagonal slices.
8. In a preheated wok, add oil, garlic, and ginger. Stir-fry over medium heat until fragrant, about 10 seconds. Increase heat to high; add chicken and sausages and stir occasionally until lightly browned, about 5 minutes. Add mashed fermented bean curd to the center of the wok; toss with chicken mixture.
9. Add mushrooms, lily buds, and green onions and stir-fry for 30 seconds.
10. Mix cornstarch with chicken stock, wine, and pepper to a smooth mixture. Pour into the wok and stir until sauce thickens, about 10 seconds. Remove from heat.
11. Place clay pot over medium-high heat. Boil rice and stir with chopsticks to loosen grains. Continue boiling until surface water is absorbed (about 5 minutes). Pour chicken mixture over rice, cover, reduce heat to low, and cook for 20 minutes. Turn off heat; do not remove lid; let sit for 10 minutes longer.
